title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who embarked on a creative pursuit. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Emily, Photographer
I've always considered photography as a side hobby, something I did only when I had free time. But when I decided to dedicate more time to it, I [48]realized that I could capture emotions and stories through my lens[/48]. The most surprising part was when I started sharing my work and received positive feedback, encouraging me to pursue it even more. Despite initial doubts, [49]the more I immersed myself in it, the more my confidence grew[/49]. My perspective on various subjects shifted, and I learned to see beauty in the mundane.
texts.B:
### Oliver, Musician
Taking up the violin was a challenge I eagerly embraced. I hadn't expected to settle in so fast, [47]adapting quickly to practicing daily[/47]. It was exhilarating to notice improvements week after week. Initially, facing the sheets filled with complex notes was intimidating, but [50]each small success made the effort worthwhile[/50]. Playing music started as a personal journey, but soon I found myself connecting with others who shared my passion.
texts.C:
### Mia, Ceramicist
Ceramics always fascinated me, so I enrolled in a local class. [51]The tactile nature of clay was both soothing and invigorating[/51]. I was thrilled by the opportunity to create something tangible from a lump of clay. There were moments of frustration, but [52]achieving a final piece that looked exactly as I imagined was immensely satisfying[/52]. Even when some pieces didn’t turn out as expected, [55]the process taught me resilience[/55]. Engaging with this art form became a beautiful form of expression.
texts.D:
### Lucas, Writer
Writing has been my escape and my challenge. [53]The most rewarding aspect was witnessing my characters come alive on the page[/53]. Drafting a story was daunting at first, but [54]once I developed a routine, the words flowed more easily[/54]. Although the initial feedback wasn’t all positive, I chose to [56]view criticism as a path to growth[/56]. Sharing my work was nerve-wracking, yet connecting with readers and receiving their perspectives made it fulfilling beyond measure.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by how quickly they adapted to a new routine?
questions.explanation: B says: 'I hadn't expected to settle in so fast, adapting quickly to practicing daily.'

questions.question: mentions realizing they could convey emotions through their work?
questions.explanation: A expresses: 'realized that I could capture emotions and stories through my lens.'

questions.question: mentions gaining confidence with increased engagement?
questions.explanation: A notes: 'the more I immersed myself in it, the more my confidence grew.'

questions.question: describes small successes making the effort worthwhile?
questions.explanation: B shares: 'each small success made the effort worthwhile.'

questions.question: expresses feeling both soothed and invigorated by the tactile nature of their work?
questions.explanation: C states: 'The tactile nature of clay was both soothing and invigorating.'

questions.question: describes feeling satisfied by achieving a vision?
questions.explanation: C describes: 'achieving a final piece that looked exactly as I imagined was immensely satisfying.'

questions.question: mentions their characters coming alive in their work?
questions.explanation: D highlights: 'The most rewarding aspect was witnessing my characters come alive on the page.'

questions.question: mentions finding a routine improved their productivity?
questions.explanation: D indicates: 'once I developed a routine, the words flowed more easily.'

questions.question: mentions learning resilience through failures?
questions.explanation: C remarks: 'the process taught me resilience.'

questions.question: sees criticism as an opportunity for growth?
questions.explanation: D concludes: 'view criticism as a path to growth.'
